title: ProductStandardTermAndConditionTypeValues
description: Standard Terms and Conditions in a Banking Product Catalogue

x-enumeration-literals.name: Product General Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description: A value of Product TermAndCondition Type whereby product general terms and conditions will be defined.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Product Context Specific Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description: A value of Product TermAndCondition Type whereby product context specific terms and conditions (e.g., regulation, market, sale) will be defined.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Product Combination Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Banks often offer product combinations (also called bundled products, composite products, or cross-sell packages)-and these do typically come with their own set of terms and conditions in addition to the standard T&Cs of each individual product.

Eligibility for the Combo
Customer must meet eligibility for each individual product
Sometimes limited to specific customer segments (e.g., salaried, NRIs, SMEs)

2)   Bundled Benefits
Preferential pricing (e.g., discounted interest rate on loan)
Waiver of certain fees (e.g., annual fee on credit card if salary account is active)
Linked rewards or loyalty programs

3)   Conditional Benefits
Benefits may apply only if all products are active

4)  Cross-Product Dependencies

Closure or default of one product (e.g., closing savings account) may:
Trigger loss of benefit on other products
Cause re-pricing of interest rates
Lead to automatic closure or cancellation of linked product(s)

x-enumeration-literals.name: Product Pricing and Fee Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Interest rates (fixed/variable; APR for loans)

Fee structure:

Account opening fee
Maintenance charges
Prepayment/foreclosure charges
Processing fees (for loans/credit)

Penalties (e.g., late payment, insufficient funds)

x-enumeration-literals.name: Product Matching Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description: A Product Matching Condition is a set of predefined eligibility and suitability criteria used to identify and recommend products or bundled offerings that align with a customers profile, needs, and risk tolerance.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Product Eligibility Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Minimum age requirement
Residency/citizenship
Income/employment conditions (for credit products)
KYC compliance

x-enumeration-literals.name: Product Repayment Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
EMI schedule
Grace period
Early payment terms
Interest calculation method (reducing balance, flat rate)

x-enumeration-literals.name: Product Documentation Required Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Proof of identity
Proof of address
Income documents (if applicable)
Product-specific forms
